behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff (three collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary step), not single-function logic. REAL-INFRA is not required by behavior text; this can be caught with in-process source adapters/fakes. Rejected System because behavior text is about payload composition, not real network/OS behavior. Rejected Agentic because it does not evaluate model output quality, only pre-summary input completeness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/report modules with stubbed source clients returning distinct sentinel values for metrics, issues, and deployment logs; assert the exact payload passed into the summary-generation interface contains all three sections/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module interaction boundary: source error propagation into report assembly and graceful degradation. In-process failure simulation (exception/timeout from one adapter) is sufficient. Rejected System because behavior text does not require validating real HTTP timeout semantics, only fallback behavior. Rejected Agentic because correctness is fallback note insertion, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run reporting pipeline modules together with one source client configured to fail and two returning data; assert run completes, report contains a `data unavailable` marker only for failed section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model’s generated content fidelity (LLM-DEP). A stubbed summarizer would hide hallucination/omission errors and always pass deterministic checks. Rejected Workflow because behavior describes one generation decision, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative weekly input data to the real summary-generation model and evaluate output against key-value fidelity checks (e.g., required metric/issue/deploy facts must be present and numerically consistent) over repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external APIs and real email transport/inbox delivery (REAL-INFRA). In-process mocks can hide SMTP/auth/routing/inbox issues. Rejected Agentic because this behavior’s asserted outcome is end-to-end delivery, not summary quality; model output can be fixed/test fixture content for this test.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled job end-to-end against reachable test instances of the three data sources and a real email delivery path to a test mailbox; assert email is received by configured recipient with expected subject/body/attachments and source data sections present.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is orchestration/state handoff: email failure -> local persistence -> retry on subsequent run. This can be reproduced with in-process email client stub raising unreachable errors plus real local filesystem state across two invocations. Rejected System because behavior text does not require validating real SMTP/network stack behavior, only fallback+retry semantics. Rejected Workflow because no multiple model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke run 1 with email sender mocked to fail; assert report is persisted to local file/spool. Invoke run 2 with sender restored; assert persisted report is retried and sent, and spool is cleared/marked delivered.
